cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the correct answer to this question. The question is asking what can be learned from the text provided. The options are A, B, C, and D. It's a 47-unit living building where two-thirds of the residents are formerly homeless people, and the rest are low-income families. The building has a clean and cool boiler room with special equipment that produces energy for electricity and heat, reusing heat that would otherwise be lost. This reduces carbon emissions and cuts costs. Then the text mentions that Fox Point is operated by Palladia, and they got support from ECP (Enterprise Community Partners). ECP has created Green Communities Standards for healthy, environmentally clever, and affordable homes. These standards include water conservation, energy saving, and using eco-friendly materials. Even though it increases construction costs by 2%, the savings from lower running costs pay it back quickly. Michael Bloomberg, the mayor of New York, plans to create 165,000 affordable housing units for 500,000 New Yorkers. The text states that almost 80% of the city's greenhouse gas emissions come from buildings, and 40% of those are from housing. Because of this, the city's DHPD (Department of Housing and Preservation and Development) will require all new projects to follow ECP's green standards. The text also mentions that similar measures are taken by other cities like Cleveland and Denver, but New York's DHPD is the largest city developer of affordable housing in the country.
